name: London
imageAlt: Big Ben and London skyline in fog
description: On October 2, 1872, the methodical Phileas Fogg wagers £20,000 at the Reform Club that he can circumnavigate the globe in exactly 80 days. Accompanied by his newly hired French valet Passepartout, he departs London by train at 8:45 PM.
event: £20,000 staked. The world in 80 days. They think him mad.
narration: On the evening of October second, eighteen seventy-two, Phileas Fogg makes the most audacious wager in the history of the Reform Club. Twenty thousand pounds — his entire fortune — that he can circle the globe in exactly eighty days. The gentlemen of the club think him utterly mad. With his newly hired valet Passepartout at his side, Fogg departs London by train at precisely eight forty-five PM.
logEntry: Departed Reform Club 8:45 PM. £20,000 wagered. 80 days begins.

name: Paris
imageAlt: Paris at night with dramatic lighting
description: Fogg and Passepartout pass through Paris via the Northern Railway. They do not stop to sightsee — Fogg is a man of strict schedules. Meanwhile, Detective Fix begins his pursuit, convinced Fogg is the Bank of England robber.
event: Detective Fix intercepts a telegram. He's convinced Fogg is the bank robber. The chase begins.
narration: As Fogg passes through Paris without even glancing at the Eiffel Tower, a Scotland Yard detective named Fix intercepts a telegram. A daring robbery at the Bank of England — fifty-five thousand pounds stolen. And this mysterious Fogg, fleeing eastward with a fortune? Fix is certain he has his man. The hunt begins.
logEntry: Paris — Northern Railway. No stop. Detective Fix begins pursuit.

name: Turin
description: The train crosses the Alps through the Mont Cenis tunnel into Italy. Fogg remains unperturbed, playing whist while Passepartout marvels at the stunning mountain scenery rushing past the windows.
event: Eight miles of darkness through Mont Cenis — then the Alps explode into view.
narration: The locomotive plunges into the eight-mile Mont Cenis tunnel, cutting straight through the heart of the Alps. When daylight returns, the scene is breathtaking — snow-capped peaks towering on every side as they descend into Italy. Passepartout presses his face to the glass in wonder. Fogg doesn't look up from his card game.
logEntry: Mont Cenis tunnel — Alps crossed by rail. Descending into Italy.

name: Brindisi
imageAlt: Mediterranean coastal town with colorful buildings
description: Arriving at the port city of Brindisi in southern Italy, Fogg and Passepartout board the P&O steamer Mongolia bound for Suez. They are precisely on schedule — not a minute early, not a minute late.
event: The steamer Mongolia awaits. 1,310 tons of iron and steam, bound for the Orient.
narration: At the sun-drenched port of Brindisi, the mighty P and O steamer Mongolia sits waiting. Thirteen hundred tons of iron and steam, ready to carry them across the Mediterranean toward the Orient. Fogg checks his watch — they're on time to the second. Not a minute early. Not a minute late.
logEntry: Brindisi port. Boarded steamer Mongolia — bound for Suez.

name: Suez
imageAlt: Pyramids of Giza under dramatic desert sky
description: Passing through the newly opened Suez Canal, Fogg arrives exactly on schedule. Detective Fix, now stationed at Suez, telegraphs London for an arrest warrant and decides to follow Fogg to Bombay.
event: Fix wires London: 'Send warrant immediately.' But the warrant doesn't come...
narration: Through the newly opened Suez Canal they pass, arriving exactly on schedule. Detective Fix is already here, waiting. He fires off an urgent telegram to London: 'Send arrest warrant immediately. Suspect heading east. Will shadow to Bombay.' But the days pass, and the warrant does not come.
logEntry: Suez Canal passage. Fix wires London — no warrant received.

name: Aden
imageAlt: Sand dunes overlooking the ocean in Yemen
description: A brief coaling stop at this British outpost on the Arabian Peninsula. The Mongolia refuels while Fix continues shadowing Fogg, growing increasingly frustrated at the lack of an arrest warrant from London.
event: 175 tons of coal devoured. Fix paces the dock, cursing London's slow bureaucrats.
narration: A brief coaling stop at this sweltering British outpost on the Arabian Peninsula. The Mongolia's furnaces devour a hundred and seventy-five tons of coal while Fix paces the dock in the brutal heat, cursing the slow bureaucrats of London. Where is that blasted warrant?
logEntry: Aden coaling stop. Mongolia refueled. Fix still without warrant.

name: Bombay
imageAlt: Gateway of India monument at Mumbai waterfront
description: Arriving in Bombay (Mumbai), Fogg is half a day ahead of schedule. Passepartout, wandering the city, enters a pagoda and is beaten for desecrating a holy place by wearing shoes inside. They board the Great Indian Peninsula Railway.
event: Passepartout enters a sacred pagoda with his shoes on. Seized by furious priests!
narration: Bombay! Half a day ahead of schedule. But Passepartout, wandering the city in wonder, stumbles into a sacred pagoda with his shoes still on — an unforgivable sacrilege. Furious priests seize him, beat him, and drag him before a magistrate. Fogg pays a hefty fine to free his foolish valet, and they race to board the Great Indian Peninsula Railway.
logEntry: Bombay. Half day ahead. Passepartout fined for pagoda sacrilege.

name: Calcutta
imageAlt: Howrah Bridge in Kolkata at dusk
description: The railway ends 50 miles short! Fogg purchases an elephant for £2,000 and hires a guide. En route through the jungle, they discover a procession — the beautiful Princess Aouda is about to be burned alive in a suttee ritual. Fogg mounts a daring rescue.
event: Passepartout rises from the funeral pyre — Aouda is snatched from the flames!
narration: The railway ends fifty miles short of Calcutta! Fogg purchases an elephant for two thousand pounds and plunges into the jungle. There, they stumble upon a horrifying procession — the beautiful Princess Aouda is about to be burned alive in a suttee ritual. In a stroke of mad genius, Passepartout disguises himself as the dead rajah and rises from the funeral pyre. In the chaos, they snatch Aouda from certain death and flee on the elephant through the jungle!
logEntry: Railway ended short. Elephant purchased. Princess Aouda rescued from suttee.

name: Hong Kong
imageAlt: Hong Kong harbor and skyline at sunrise
description: In Hong Kong, the treacherous Detective Fix lures Passepartout to a waterfront tavern. He slips opium into his drink, rendering him unconscious. Passepartout barely staggers aboard the steamer Carnatic — but without Fogg!
event: Fix drugs Passepartout with opium! The Carnatic sails — without Fogg.
narration: In Hong Kong, Detective Fix shows his true colours. He lures Passepartout to a waterfront tavern and slips opium into his drink. The valet collapses in a daze. He barely staggers aboard the steamer Carnatic — but Fogg and Aouda are left stranded on the dock. Fix smirks in the shadows. The Carnatic sails without them.
logEntry: Hong Kong. Passepartout drugged by Fix. Carnatic sailed without Fogg!

name: Yokohama
imageAlt: Yokohama cityscape with ferris wheel at twilight
description: Fogg charters a pilot boat and battles through typhoon-force winds across the China Sea to reach Yokohama. Against all odds, he finds Passepartout performing as a clown in a Japanese acrobat troupe — his only way to earn passage home!
event: Passepartout discovered mid-performance — stacked in a human pyramid, wearing wings!
narration: Fogg charters a pilot boat and battles through typhoon-force winds across the China Sea. Against all odds, he reaches Yokohama. And there, in the most unlikely of places, he finds Passepartout — performing as a clown in a Japanese acrobat troupe, stacked in a human pyramid, wearing a ridiculous costume with wings! It was the only way the poor valet could earn passage home.
logEntry: Yokohama. Chartered pilot boat through typhoon. Passepartout found in circus.

name: San Francisco
imageAlt: Golden Gate Bridge in fog at sunset
description: After 22 grueling days crossing the vast Pacific Ocean, the party arrives in San Francisco. They stumble into a violent political rally between supporters of rival candidates — fists, clubs, and chaos everywhere! Fogg punches a man to protect Aouda.
event: A full-scale riot erupts! Fogg floors Colonel Proctor with a single punch.
narration: Twenty-two grueling days crossing the vast Pacific Ocean, and at last — America! But San Francisco greets them with chaos. A violent political rally erupts into a full-scale street riot. When Colonel Proctor insults Aouda, Fogg floors him with a single, devastating punch. Three thousand, seven hundred and eighty-six miles of wild American frontier now lie ahead by rail.
logEntry: San Francisco. 22 days Pacific crossing. Street brawl — Fogg punches Colonel Proctor.

name: New York
imageAlt: Historical New York harbor with ships and city skyline
description: The most harrowing leg yet — Sioux warriors attack the train at full speed! Passepartout crawls under the cars to uncouple the engine, is captured, and must be rescued by cavalry. Then the bridge at Medicine Bow is collapsing — the train leaps across at maximum steam!
event: Sioux attack! Bridge collapse! And the steamer sails — 45 minutes too late.
narration: The most harrowing leg of the entire journey! Sioux warriors attack the train at full speed! Passepartout crawls beneath the thundering cars to uncouple the engine, is captured, and must be rescued by a cavalry charge. Then the bridge at Medicine Bow is collapsing under them — the train leaps across at maximum steam! And after all that — Fogg reaches New York harbour only to watch the steamer China pull away from the dock. Forty-five minutes too late.
logEntry: Sioux attack. Bridge collapse. Kidnapping. Reached New York — steamer gone.

name: Liverpool
imageAlt: Liverpool docks with ships and historic buildings
description: Running out of coal mid-Atlantic, Fogg purchases the entire ship from its captain and orders the crew to burn the wooden superstructure — masts, cabins, decks, everything! They limp into Liverpool as a floating wreck, with hours to spare.
event: Fix slaps on the handcuffs! 'You are under arrest!' Hours tick away in a cell...
narration: Out of coal in the middle of the Atlantic, Fogg buys the entire ship and orders the crew to burn the wooden superstructure — masts, cabins, decks, everything! They limp into Liverpool as a floating wreck. But the moment Fogg steps ashore, Detective Fix slaps him in handcuffs. 'You are under arrest for robbery!' Hours tick away in a prison cell... until word arrives that the real thief was caught three days ago. Fogg is free — but has he lost the wager?
logEntry: Liverpool. Burned the ship for fuel. Arrested by Fix — then released. Real thief caught.

name: London
imageAlt: Big Ben and Thames River reflection at golden sunset
description: Fogg believes all is lost. Sitting in silent despair at his Savile Row home, he calculates he is one day late. But Passepartout bursts in screaming — by traveling EASTWARD around the globe, they gained a day crossing the International Date Line! It is December 20th, not the 21st! Fogg leaps into a cab and races to the Reform Club...
event: 'Here I am, gentlemen!' — with seconds to spare!
narration: All seems lost. Fogg sits in silent despair, calculating that he is one day late. But then — Passepartout bursts through the door screaming! By traveling eastward around the globe, they gained a day crossing the International Date Line! It is December the twentieth, not the twenty-first! Fogg leaps into a cab and races through the streets of London. He bursts through the doors of the Reform Club with seconds to spare. 'Here I am, gentlemen!' He has won the wager, circled the world in eighty days, and along the way — won the heart of Aouda.
logEntry: LONDON! Gained a day via Date Line! Reform Club reached with seconds to spare. WAGER WON!
